extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH won his 31st Tour stage in a bunch sprint at Fougères, delivered by Mørkøv after Ballerini mechanical, beating PHILIPSEN and NAESEN on the line.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck lost Ballerini to mechanical in finale but pivoted to use world champion ALAPHILIPPE for final leadout pull.
PHILIPSEN nearly caught Cavendish at line suggesting UAE positioned too far back to overcome Mørkøv's textbook pilot.
VAN MOER rode for win to honor Lotto's Ewan injury, proving domestiques prioritized over regrouping sprint trains.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: First Tour stage win in 3 years at age 36 after near-retirement signals sustainable form, not fluke
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: negative: Mechanical in finale exposed Deceuninck's thin sprint depth despite having green jersey as backup
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: World champion sacrificed position to pull for Cavendish demonstrates team commitment trumping individual GC
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Nearly caught Cavendish from disadvantaged position confirms top-3 sprint speed without perfect leadout
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: positive: Delivered perfect pilot after losing Ballerini proves he's essential to Cavendish resurgence, not just complementary
rider_signals.VAN MOER Brent: neutral: Rode aggressively for Ewan after teammate's crash but lacked pure speed against top sprinters
